title: Neomicrocalamus andropogonifolius

The classification of Neomicrocalamus andropogonifolius places it in the tribe Bambuseae, which is a group of woody grasses commonly known as bamboo. Neomicrocalamus andropogonifolius is native to tropical Asia, where it grows in a variety of habitats. The specific classification of Neomicrocalamus andropogonifolius is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Poales, Family: Poaceae, Genus: Neomicrocalamus, Species: N. andropogonifolius.

intro: Neomicrocalamus andropogonifolius, also known as Indian broom grass or Andropogon grass, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neomicrocalamus andropogonifolius include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
